completed_tasks.title: Add wheel event handler with deltaY normalization and gesture lifecycle
completed_tasks.description: In PullToRefresh.svelte: (1) Add two new state variables: wheelAccumulator (number, 0) and wheelEndTimer (ReturnType<typeof setTimeout> | null). (2) Add a deltaY normalization helper that converts deltaMode LINE (x24) and PAGE (x800) to pixels. (3) Add handleWheel(event: WheelEvent) implementing the full gesture lifecycle: activation guard (isRefreshing false, isTracking false, scrollTop===0, deltaY<0), accumulation (wheelAccumulator += abs(normalizedDeltaY), pullDistance = applyPullDamping(wheelAccumulator)), preventDefault during tracking, debounce timer (150ms) for gesture end that triggers doRefresh() or retract, and reverse-scroll cancellation (deltaY>0 cancels and retracts). (4) Register the wheel listener in onMount with { passive: false } and remove in cleanup. (5) Clear wheelEndTimer in onDestroy.
completed_tasks.done_when: Wheel gesture activates at scrollTop===0 with deltaY<0, accumulates damped pull distance, ends via 150ms debounce, triggers doRefresh() above threshold or retracts below it, cancels on reverse scroll, normalizes deltaMode, and cleans up timer on destroy. Existing touch and pointer handlers unchanged.

completed_tasks.title: Add unit tests for wheel gesture lifecycle
completed_tasks.description: In PullToRefresh.test.ts, add a new describe('PullToRefresh wheel gesture') block with tests covering: (1) gesture triggers refresh past threshold (simulate wheel events, advance debounce timer, verify refresh event), (2) retracts below threshold, (3) scroll guard (no activation when scrollTop>0), (4) reverse scroll cancels gesture, (5) debounce timer fires settle after 150ms, (6) debounce timer resets on new wheel event within 150ms, (7) no activation during isRefreshing, (8) no concurrent gesture with pointer/touch (isTracking guard), (9) deltaMode normalization (LINE and PAGE modes produce reasonable pull distances). Use vi.useFakeTimers() and vi.advanceTimersByTime() for debounce testing. Simulate wheel events via fireEvent.wheel() or dispatchEvent(new WheelEvent(...)).
completed_tasks.done_when: All 9 unit test cases pass. Tests cover the full wheel gesture lifecycle including edge cases. No existing touch or pointer tests broken.

completed_tasks.title: Add E2E test for wheel PTR gesture
completed_tasks.description: In pull-to-refresh.spec.ts, add a new describe('PTR wheel gesture') block with at least one @smoke-tagged test: use Playwright page.mouse.wheel(0, negativeDeltaY) to simulate wheel events at scrollTop===0, verify the PTR indicator becomes visible (content translates down), then verify the indicator animates out after the debounce settle. Ensure the test runs in Chromium and does not interfere with existing touch/pointer E2E tests.
completed_tasks.done_when: E2E test passes in Chromium: wheel gesture shows PTR indicator, triggers refresh or retract, and indicator dismisses. Tagged @smoke. Existing E2E tests unaffected.

completed_tasks.title: QA fixes: scrollTop <= 0 guard in handleWheel + DOM_DELTA_PAGE unit test
completed_tasks.description: Two QA-requested fixes: (1) In PullToRefresh.svelte handleWheel(), change the activation guard from scrollTop === 0 to scrollTop <= 0 (line ~393). Sub-pixel scroll positions from trackpad momentum can prevent gesture activation. Touch and pointer handlers already use <= 0. This is a one-character fix. (2) In PullToRefresh.test.ts, add a unit test for DOM_DELTA_PAGE (deltaMode 2, multiply by 800). The exec plan acceptance criteria require page-mode normalization coverage, but only LINE mode is currently tested.
completed_tasks.done_when: handleWheel uses scrollTop <= 0 in its activation guard. A new unit test for DOM_DELTA_PAGE mode exists and passes, verifying that deltaMode 2 produces a pull distance consistent with an 800x multiplier. All existing tests still pass.

history.note: Feature initiated: Add trackpad/wheel gesture support to PTR so desktop trackpad users can scroll-up past the top to trigger refresh. Continues work on feat/ptr-desktop-viewport branch, which already has mouse click-and-drag PTR support.
Advanced to discovery. Routing to product-manager for requirements gathering and exec plan creation.

Product Manager completed discovery. Exec plan written to docs/exec-plans/active/2026-04-05-ptr-trackpad-wheel.md. All sections complete: goal, non-goals, constraints, current state, proposed approach, alternatives, risks, acceptance criteria (18 criteria), test plan, rollout, design/tasks placeholders, decision log.
Advanced to design. Routing to principal-engineer for technical design.

Advanced to tasks. Design section in exec plan was left as placeholder by PE, but the Proposed Approach section contains full technical design (event wiring, gesture lifecycle, state variables, deltaY normalization, test plan). Broke work into 3 tasks: T1 (wheel handler implementation), T2 (unit tests), T3 (E2E test).
Starting implementation of T1: Add wheel event handler with deltaY normalization and gesture lifecycle in PullToRefresh.svelte
Starting implementation of T2: Add unit tests for wheel gesture lifecycle in PullToRefresh.test.ts
Starting implementation of T3: Add E2E test for wheel PTR gesture in pull-to-refresh.spec.ts
All 3 tasks built (T1 wheel handler, T2 unit tests, T3 E2E test). Routing to build-specialist for quality gate verification.
Routing to quality-assurance for code review of all changes in ptr-trackpad-wheel feature.
QA returned REQUEST CHANGES with two fixes: (1) scrollTop === 0 -> <= 0 in handleWheel, (2) add DOM_DELTA_PAGE unit test. Created T4 to bundle both fixes. Routing to software-developer.
All 4 tasks built (T1-T4 including QA fixes). Routing to build-specialist for quality gate verification.
Re-review after QA-requested fixes (T4: scrollTop <= 0 guard + DOM_DELTA_PAGE unit test). Routing to quality-assurance for focused re-review.
All 4 tasks (T1-T4) complete and verified. Build passed, QA approved. Moving to acceptance. Routing to product-manager for acceptance criteria validation.
